conditions: Major depressive disorder (recurrent, in partial remission)
medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for acute anxiety
Melatonin 3 mg at night as needed
health.mental_health: Symptoms fluctuate with retirement planning and uncertainty about income; he’s in therapy for anxiety/depression and has periodic panic symptoms before high-stakes performances or financial meetings. He keeps this private from most students and parents.
recent_visits: Telehealth psychiatry medication check-in (6 weeks ago)
Weekly individual therapy session (ongoing; last session this week)
Primary care annual physical with labs (3 months ago)
ENT visit for tinnitus/hearing screen related to teaching volume (5 months ago)

finance.debt: Carries a remaining car loan and a small personal loan used during a low-enrollment period; he is trying to pay both down before retiring. He also has an outstanding balance on a 0% introductory credit card that will start accruing interest later this year.
investments: 529 plan beneficiary contribution for a niece (small, occasional)
recent_transactions: Paid $1,250 to a fee-only financial planner for a retirement readiness plan
Purchased refurbished Yamaha digital piano for studio use ($980)
Paid quarterly estimated taxes to IRS ($1,900)
Paid therapy copays and psychiatry visit balance ($210 total last month)

relationships: Long-term partner: Miguel Reyes (they keep their relationship relatively private with some clients)
Older sister: Priya Novak (lives in Atlanta; frequent calls about family and retirement decisions)
Close friend and fellow musician: Hannah Blevins (occasional gigs and emotional support)
Former mentor: Dr. Alan Whitaker (retired professor; advising on winding down teaching)
A small circle of adult students he’s taught for years (some are emotionally invested in his retirement timeline)
social.social_media_activity: Uses Instagram and Facebook mainly to post recital photos (with consent), studio announcements, and occasional short practice tips; avoids discussing mental health publicly and is cautious about sharing his retirement plans broadly to prevent student attrition.
private_conversations: Discussing whether to disclose anxiety/depression accommodations to select students and parents
Conversations with partner about marriage, health insurance coverage after retirement, and budgeting
Talking with sister about drafting a will and choosing a healthcare proxy
Exploring whether to sell the studio brand or quietly reduce the roster over the next 12–18 months

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Thu 1:00 pm–8:00 pm teaching lessons; Sat 10:00 am–2:00 pm for make-ups and adult students; Fridays reserved for admin, practice, and occasional sessions.
regular_appointments: Biweekly meeting with financial planner (for the next 3 months)
Choir rehearsal as accompanist (Wednesdays 7:00 pm–9:00 pm, seasonal commitment)
upcoming_events: Spring studio recital at Belmont University neighborhood venue (in 3 weeks)
Meeting with CPA about estimated taxes and retirement transition (next Friday)
Weekend trip to Gatlinburg with partner as a “pre-retirement reset” (in 6 weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Mornings are quiet: coffee, 30–45 minutes of practice, admin emails and billing, then errands or paperwork. Afternoons/evenings are back-to-back lessons with short breaks; he decompresses late at night with reading or TV, which can worsen insomnia during stressful weeks.

professional.company: Self-employed private studio operating as 'Novak Music Studio' (home-based with a small rented rehearsal room for recitals)
projects: Planning a gradual studio handoff: identifying a trusted teacher to take over select students
Creating a retirement timeline and communication plan for families (12–18 months)
Building a digital library of technique videos for students to use after he reduces hours
Preparing advanced students for Tennessee All-State auditions
professional.performance_issues: During high-anxiety periods he struggles with sleep and administrative follow-through (invoicing, rescheduling, emails). He worries about reputation impact if families notice inconsistency, so he is quietly reducing new student intake rather than announcing a firm retirement date.
workplace_relationships: Friendly but careful relationship with a local instrument shop (Cumberland Music Supply) that refers students—he hasn’t told them his retirement timeline
Occasional collaboration with church music directors who hire him as an accompanist; he limits gigs to avoid burnout
A few parents push for extra lessons and performance opportunities, increasing pressure as he plans to scale down

hobbies: Hiking at Radnor Lake and Shelby Bottoms greenway
Cooking simple Indian and Tex-Mex meals
Reading music biographies and psychology books
Attending small venue shows in East Nashville (selectively, to manage sensory overload)
habits: Uses a weighted blanket and guided breathing app at night to manage anxiety
Drinks 2–3 cups of coffee daily and tries to cut off by early afternoon
Keeps a private journal about retirement fears and mood tracking
Occasionally uses a low-dose THC gummy on weekends for sleep (not shared with most people)
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with his partner in a rented two-bedroom duplex in Nashville; one room is set up as a teaching studio. They’re considering downsizing or moving to a quieter suburb once he retires and no longer needs steady foot traffic for lessons.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked, moderate-carb; tries to limit alcohol and late-night heavy meals to help sleep. Uses seasonal allergy-friendly strategies (air purifier, avoiding open-window nights in peak pollen).
